title: leontodon protheiformis subsp. incanus
intro: Leontodon protheiformis subsp. incanus, also known as the hoary hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing relief from gastrointestinal problems. Leontodon protheiformis subsp. incanus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
